daily_brief: The world holds its breath as President Trump's midnight deadline for Iran approaches, with the US threatening to destroy all bridges and power plants if Tehran does not reopen the Strait of Hormuz [BBC], [France 24], [Al Jazeera]. The EU has warned such strikes on civilian infrastructure would be illegal, while the UN Security Council prepares to vote on a watered-down resolution addressing the crisis [RFI]. In a notable display of transatlantic political alignment, Vice President JD Vance has arrived in Hungary to rally support for Prime Minister Viktor Orbán ahead of Sunday's parliamentary election, where polls show the nationalist leader trailing opposition challenger Péter Magyar [BBC], [France 24], [The Hindu]. Meanwhile, Taiwan's opposition leader Cheng Li-wun has begun a historic visit to China, the first by an incumbent KMT chair in a decade, signalling shifting cross-strait dynamics amid uncertainty over US Taiwan policy [BBC], [Financial Times]. NASA's Artemis II mission reached a historic milestone as astronauts entered the Moon's gravitational sphere, preparing for humanity's first lunar flyby since 1972 [RFI], [Al Jazeera]. In Australia, war hero Ben Roberts-Smith was arrested on five counts of war crime murder relating to killings of unarmed Afghan civilians [Al Jazeera], [The Hindu].

top_stories.headline: Trump's Iran Ultimatum Enters Final Hours as US Threatens to Destroy Civilian Infrastructure
bullets: President Trump has set a Tuesday 8pm (Washington time) deadline for Iran to agree to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face US military strikes on all bridges and power plants

Trump stated he was 'not at all' concerned about potential war crimes, while legal experts and UN officials warned that deliberately targeting civilian infrastructure violates international humanitarian law
Iran has rejected a 45-day ceasefire proposal from Pakistan and issued its own demands; the IRGC declared the strait has undergone 'irreversible strategic changes'
The UN Security Council will vote Tuesday morning on a watered-down resolution addressing the Hormuz blockade, having dropped language authorising the use of force

sources.name: BBC
sources.framing_note: Emphasises diplomatic positioning and Trump's credibility challenges if he extends deadline again

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Leads with war crimes angle and expert legal analysis

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Highlights Iran's perspective and questions why Iran would trust US given track record

sources.name: RFI
sources.framing_note: Focuses on EU response and humanitarian concerns

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Emphasises Pakistan's mediation efforts and UN alarm

sources.name: RNZ
sources.framing_note: New Zealand angle on fuel shock and PM's criticism of US goals as 'unclear'

top_stories.headline: Vice President Vance Arrives in Hungary to Rally for Orbán Days Before Pivotal Election
bullets: JD Vance flew to Budapest to address a campaign rally with Prime Minister Viktor Orbán, who faces his toughest election challenge in nearly 40 years of political life
Most independent polls show Orbán's Fidesz party trailing the centre-right Tisza party led by Péter Magyar by 10-20 percentage points ahead of the April 12 vote
Trump previously gave Orbán his 'complete and total support' and granted Hungary an exemption from US sanctions on Russian oil companies Rosneft and Lukoil
The visit represents the Trump administration's broader strategy of supporting like-minded right-wing leaders in Europe

sources.name: BBC
sources.framing_note: Provides extensive context on Orbán's 16-year rule and EU tensions

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Notes Vance is 'one of the fiercest critics of centrist and progressive European governments'

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Frames as example of Trump 'propping up like-minded right-wing leaders'

top_stories.headline: Taiwan Opposition Leader Begins Historic China Visit Amid Cross-Strait Tensions
bullets: KMT chairperson Cheng Li-wun arrived in China for a six-day visit, the first by an incumbent opposition leader in a decade, after accepting President Xi Jinping's invitation
Cheng said she hopes to be a 'bridge for peace' and is expected to meet Xi during stops in Shanghai, Nanjing and Beijing
The visit comes amid growing scepticism about US reliability in Taiwan following Trump's mixed signals on Taiwan policy
Beijing cut communications with Taiwan after President Tsai Ing-wen took office in 2016; analysts note Cheng's eagerness contrasts with predecessors' more cautious approach

sources.name: BBC
sources.framing_note: Focuses on Taiwan domestic politics and analyst views on Cheng's motivations

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Highlights timing ahead of planned Trump summit

top_stories.headline: Artemis II Astronauts Enter Moon's Gravitational Sphere in Historic Deep Space Mission
bullets: NASA's Orion spacecraft crossed into the Moon's sphere of influence, where lunar gravity now exerts stronger pull than Earth's
The four-person crew will conduct humanity's first lunar flyby since Apollo 17 in 1972, travelling farther from Earth than any humans in history
Victor Glover will become the first person of colour to orbit the Moon, Christina Koch the first woman, and Jeremy Hansen the first non-American on such a journey
The lunar observation period begins Tuesday morning, with the crew expected to witness the Moon eclipsing the Sun

sources.name: RFI
sources.framing_note: Emphasises scientific milestones and historic firsts

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Focuses on the distance record being broken

sources.name: RNZ
sources.framing_note: Provides detailed schedule for New Zealand viewers

top_stories.headline: Australia Arrests War Hero Ben Roberts-Smith on Five Counts of War Crime Murder
bullets: Ben Roberts-Smith, 47, Australia's most decorated living soldier and Victoria Cross recipient, was arrested at Sydney Airport
He faces five counts of war crime murder relating to unarmed Afghan civilians killed between 2009 and 2012 during his six tours of Afghanistan
Police allege victims were detained and under ADF control when killed, either shot by Roberts-Smith or by subordinates acting on his orders
The arrest follows his failed 2023 defamation case against journalists who reported he was involved in murders of unarmed Afghan men

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Leads with the arrest and references prior defamation case loss

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Emphasises his status as national hero and military honours

additional_stories.headline: Israeli strike kills 10 at Gaza school shelter as WHO suspends evacuations after aid worker death
Nine Indian police officers sentenced to death for fatal Covid custody beating of father and son
Vietnam's To Lam elected president, cementing his position as most powerful leader in decades
English resident doctors begin six-day strike after rejecting government pay offer
Pope Leo XIV delivers first Easter Mass, calling on world leaders to choose peace
Burkina Faso's military ruler says democracy is 'not for us' and elections are off the agenda
Uganda receives first US deportation flight under third-country agreement
IEA chief says current oil crisis worse than 1973, 1979 and 2002 combined
Bill Ackman's Pershing Square offers to buy Universal Music Group, potentially shifting listing from Amsterdam to New York
Cyclone Vaianu cuts off roads and closes schools across Fiji's Western Division
